hero.headline: Amity Online MBA in Digital Marketing Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved specialisation. 12 spec subjects across Sem 3-4. NAAC A+, NIRF #22.

sections.tldr: Amity University Online MBA in Digital Marketing Management covers brand management, performance marketing, social media, marketing analytics, and digital commerce across 12 Sem 3-4 subjects. NAAC A+, NIRF #22 at ₹2,25,000. Suited for digital marketers targeting marketing manager or growth lead roles.

about.body:
Digital Marketing Management is one of the fastest-growing MBA specialisations globally and in India, driven by the shift of marketing budgets toward digital channels and the demand for marketing professionals who combine channel execution with strategy and analytics. The Amity Online MBA in Digital Marketing Management covers brand management through digital channels, performance marketing, social media strategy, customer analytics, digital commerce, and marketing technology — all taught in a business management context.

The specialisation is designed for marketing executives, digital marketing specialists, and growth professionals who want to formalise their expertise and move into marketing leadership roles. It is also sought by brand managers wanting to add digital capability and by non-marketing professionals entering digital marketing from adjacent functions.

Amity University Online is NAAC A+ accredited with NIRF #22 University ranking for 2025. The Digital Marketing Management MBA carries the Amity University name on the final certificate. EdifyEdu verifies accreditation data independently and does not receive commission from Amity. Full programme overview at /universities/amity-university-online/mba.

sem3Subjects.name: Digital Customer Analytics
sem3Subjects.description: Analyse customer behaviour data from web, app, and CRM sources to drive acquisition and retention strategy.

sem3Subjects.name: Digital Journey with Brand Management
sem3Subjects.description: Map the consumer digital journey and apply brand strategy across touchpoints from awareness to advocacy.

sem3Subjects.name: Minor Project
sem3Subjects.description: Apply digital marketing tools to a real brand campaign challenge with measurable KPIs and documented results.

sem3Subjects.name: Performance Marketing
sem3Subjects.description: Design and optimise paid media campaigns across Google Ads, Meta Ads, and programmatic platforms.

sem3Subjects.name: Professional Ethics
sem3Subjects.description: Examine ethical dimensions of data-driven marketing, ad targeting, privacy regulations, and influencer disclosure.

sem3Subjects.name: Social Media and Technology Marketing
sem3Subjects.description: Build social media strategies and leverage MarTech stacks for community management and content amplification.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply strategic frameworks to digital business models, competitive positioning, and marketing ROI decisions.

sem4Subjects.name: Digital Commerce - Reinventing Business Models
sem4Subjects.description: Examine D2C, marketplace, and subscription models transforming retail and service businesses digitally.

sem4Subjects.name: Digital Marketing
sem4Subjects.description: Integrate SEO, email marketing, content strategy, and marketing automation into a unified digital plan.

sem4Subjects.name: Major Project
sem4Subjects.description: Execute an end-to-end digital marketing strategy project for a real brand with measurable business outcomes.

sem4Subjects.name: Management in Action - Social Economic and Ethical Issues
sem4Subjects.description: Analyse broader societal impacts of digital marketing practices on consumers, society, and organisations.

sem4Subjects.name: Marketing Analytics - Data Tools and Techniques
sem4Subjects.description: Apply Google Analytics 4, attribution modelling, and marketing mix modelling to measure campaign effectiveness.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1 and 2 cover the shared management core. See the main Amity MBA page for Sem 1-2 subject details.

whoHires.body:
Digital Marketing Management MBA graduates from Amity University Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** E-commerce, FMCG digital, Digital agencies, D2C brands, Technology platforms, Fintech

**Entry-level roles:** Digital Marketing Manager (junior), Growth Marketing Analyst, Performance Marketing Specialist, Social Media Manager

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Digital Marketing Manager, Head of Performance Marketing, Growth Lead, Digital Brand Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** CMO, VP Digital Marketing, Chief Growth Officer

**Top hiring organisations:** Amazon India, Flipkart, Swiggy, HUL Digital, iProspect, GroupM, Mamaearth, BOAT

skills.body:
The Digital Marketing Management specialisation builds skills that e-commerce and FMCG digital employers test in marketing manager roles:

**Technical skills:**
- Performance Marketing: Google Ads, Meta Ads Manager, bidding strategy, ROAS optimisation, and audience targeting
- Digital Customer Analytics: GA4 event tracking, cohort analysis, and customer lifetime value modelling
- Marketing Analytics: marketing mix modelling, attribution model comparison, and campaign dashboard reporting
- Brand Management in Digital Channels: brand guidelines, cross-touchpoint consistency, and programmatic brand safety

**Applied skills:**
- Social Media Strategy: content calendar management, community building, and influencer programme management
- Digital Commerce: D2C model economics, marketplace strategy, and subscription model design principles

**Soft skills:** Writing creative briefs for agencies, presenting campaign ROI to senior leadership, managing media agency and vendor relationships.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Amity Online MBA Digital Marketing Management against peers:

**vs LPU Digital Marketing MBA:** LPU offers Digital Marketing as a spec at lower fees. NAAC A++. LPU's content covers similar tools. Amity's specific 7-subject Sem 3 layout (Digital Customer Analytics, Performance Marketing, Social Media and Technology Marketing) is more granular. For candidates targeting FMCG digital and large e-commerce, Amity NIRF #22 is marginally stronger.

**vs Chandigarh University Digital Marketing MBA:** CU is NAAC A+, NIRF #19 at similar fees. CU's digital marketing placement network in north India is strong. Amity's QS ranking gives it an edge for MNC digital marketing roles. Both are solid choices at this price tier — choose based on employer geography.

**vs Manipal University Jaipur Online MBA:** Manipal Jaipur is NAAC A+ with strong brand in Rajasthan and west India. Fees are similar. Manipal Jaipur has good digital marketing content but does not offer a dedicated Digital Marketing Management spec. Amity's specialised spec with marketing analytics and digital commerce is more targeted.

fees.body:
The total program fee for the Amity University Online MBA in Digital Marketing Management is ₹2,25,000 across 4 semesters. Per-semester fee is ₹56,300. Amity does not charge a separate registration fee.

For working professionals, Amity offers a 24-month zero-cost EMI plan at approximately ₹8,906 per month through its NBFC lending partners.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Amity University Online each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The Amity University degree certificate carries the Amity University name, the MBA designation, and identifies the online learning mode per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The Digital Marketing Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode identification on the certificate is standard for UGC-DEB approved programmes and equivalent in recognition to regular MBA degrees for employment, promotions, and further studies.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Amity Online MBA Digital Marketing Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Shreya Kapoor
items.liked: Performance marketing module was hands-on with Google Ads and Meta Ads Manager live account work
items.disliked: Email marketing automation content needed more Mailchimp or HubSpot tool-specific examples
items.body: I was running Google Ads campaigns before this MBA but had no formal training on campaign strategy. The performance marketing module taught me bid strategy, audience layering, and ROAS optimisation in a structured way. I immediately applied this to increase my client ROAS by 28%. Got promoted to Digital Marketing Manager at an agency within 3 months of completing Year 2.

items.name: Nikhil Verma
items.liked: Marketing analytics module with GA4 and attribution modelling was current and directly applicable
items.disliked: Digital commerce section was somewhat theoretical — needed more platform-specific Flipkart and Amazon case content
items.body: The GA4 and marketing analytics content was the strongest section. I use marketing mix modelling insights from this module in my current growth role at an e-commerce company. The Amity degree gave me the manager-level credibility that a digital marketing certification alone could not provide. Good ROI on Rs 1.99 lakh.

items.name: Priya Sharma
items.liked: Brand management through digital channels module tied strategy to execution clearly
items.disliked: Social media module was more conceptual than tool-based — needed more platform-specific content
items.body: The digital brand management content helped me structure brand guidelines for digital channels at my company — something we had been doing intuitively. The major project I built around a brand relaunch strategy became a portfolio piece that helped me get my current Digital Brand Manager role. The Amity name was recognised by my interviewer as a strong management credential.

faqs.question: Should I choose Digital Marketing Management or Marketing and Sales Management at Amity?
faqs.answer: Choose Digital Marketing Management if you are in or targeting digital channels, performance marketing, social media, or e-commerce roles. Choose Marketing and Sales Management if you are in traditional brand management, B2B sales, FMCG field sales, or roles that combine offline and online marketing. Digital Marketing Management is more tools-heavy; Marketing and Sales is broader across channel types.

faqs.question: Does this spec cover SEO in depth?
faqs.answer: SEO is covered as part of the Digital Marketing Sem 4 subject within the context of integrated digital marketing planning. It is not a standalone SEO specialisation. Candidates targeting SEO specialist roles should supplement with Google Search Console and SEMrush certifications. The MBA covers SEO at a strategy and channel planning level, not at a technical SEO audit level.

faqs.question: Does Amity Digital Marketing MBA include Google or Meta certifications?
faqs.answer: The programme does not automatically include Google or Meta certifications. These must be pursued separately. Google offers free Google Digital Garage and Google Ads certifications; Meta offers Blueprint certifications. The MBA provides the management and strategy framework; the certifications validate platform proficiency. Both together are stronger than either alone.

faqs.question: What is the salary range for Amity Digital Marketing MBA graduates?
faqs.answer: Digital marketing professionals with 2-4 years of experience who complete this MBA typically see packages of Rs 8-16 lakh as Digital Marketing Managers. Growth leads and performance marketing heads at scale-up companies see Rs 15-25 lakh. Fresh graduates entering digital marketing typically start at Rs 4-7 lakh and advance faster than generalist MBAs in the digital marketing job market.

faqs.question: Is prior digital marketing experience required for this specialisation?
faqs.answer: No experience is required at admission. However, candidates with even 1 year of hands-on digital marketing experience extract significantly more from the performance marketing and analytics modules. Freshers should create a Google Ads or Meta Ads practice account, complete Google Digital Garage, and work on a real website or freelance project before Sem 3 starts.

chooseIf: You are in digital marketing, performance marketing, or growth roles and want a NIRF #22 management credential that covers GA4, performance marketing, and digital commerce in one spec.
You are targeting Digital Marketing Manager or Head of Digital roles at FMCG brands, e-commerce companies, or digital agencies.
You want comprehensive digital channel coverage — brand management, performance, social, commerce, analytics — rather than a single-channel focus.
consider: You want broader marketing coverage including B2B sales and FMCG offline channels — the Marketing and Sales Management spec covers more ground.
You want marketing analytics as the primary focus — consider the Business Analytics spec which offers Power BI, forecasting, and ML alongside marketing analytics.
You are targeting senior digital marketing leadership at premium companies — Manipal Online with Coursera dual certifications offers stronger brand for those roles.
